description_long: This is a dual-post metal parking sign commonly found on urban streets and sidewalks. The upper sign is white with red text, indicating 'NO PARKING' from 9 AM to 11 AM on Tuesdays, while the lower sign is white with green and blue text, designating '2 HOUR PARKING' from 8 AM to 6 PM on weekdays, with additional information and a blue 'L' symbol. The sign is mounted on two slender, dark-colored metal posts that extend vertically into the ground. The signage is rectangular and appears to be made of reflective metal or aluminum, designed for visibility and durability outdoors. The text and graphics are printed or painted onto the metal surface, with clear color differentiation for regulatory and informational purposes. The overall structure is utilitarian, weather-resistant, and intended for clear communication of parking regulations to drivers.
description: A dual-post metal parking sign with regulatory and informational panels, featuring red, green, and blue text on a white background. It displays 'NO PARKING' and '2 HOUR PARKING' instructions for urban street use.
description_view_0: Front view showing both the 'NO PARKING' and '2 HOUR PARKING' signs with all text and symbols clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side profile view showing the thinness of the sign and posts, with no visible text or graphics.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but text and graphics are less legible; the structure of the posts and sign panels is visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile view, again showing the slim profile of the sign and posts without visible signage details.
